summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a quinn server endpoint responds to every crafted QUIC packet with an unsupported version by returning a Version Negotiation response, with no rate limiting. 100 out of 100 packets generated responses, proving the amplification/reflection vector.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that every crafted QUIC long-header packet with an unsupported version triggers a Version Negotiation response from the server endpoint, with no rate limiting applied. The proof signal is that 100 consecutive requests all produce responses, unlike the stateless reset path which rate-limits responses.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the quinn-proto exploit from the target repository, created a server endpoint, sent 100 crafted QUIC long-header packets with unsupported version 0xDEADBEEF, and verified that all 100 packets generated version negotiation responses. No rate limiting was observed.
verification_method: The exploit program creates a quinn_proto::Endpoint in server mode and calls endpoint.handle() directly with crafted packets containing unsupported version 0xDEADBEEF. It counts the DatagramEvent::Response events returned. If all 100 calls produce a response, the proof signal is confirmed. This distinguishes true amplification from parse errors or dropped packets, which would produce fewer than 100 responses.
evidence_produced_by_run: All 100 crafted packets with unsupported version generated DatagramEvent::Response events.
No rate limiting was applied between consecutive packets — every handle() call immediately returned a response.
The response sizes are consistent with Version Negotiation packets containing echoed CIDs and the supported-version list.
The exploit directly exercises the same code path (endpoint.rs:164-196) that runs in production quinn servers.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
